headline: Google quietly removes privacy claims from on-device AI rollout
body: Google has removed privacy assurances previously made about its on-device AI models after deploying Gemini Nano across devices without explicit user consent. This reversal matters for healthcare AI because privacy commitments directly influence clinical adoption decisions. Healthcare organizations evaluating consumer AI tools for patient engagement or remote monitoring now face renewed uncertainty about data handling practices. As regulatory scrutiny intensifies around AI in healthcare, vendors' ability to maintain transparent privacy positioning becomes a competitive and compliance factor that healthcare buyers must carefully assess before integration into patient-facing workflows.
